pi_items.source: GCompris official screenshots page (https://www.gcompris.net/screenshots-en.html) and multiple developer sources confirm the activity is called 'Crane'
GCompris official screenshots page and documentation confirm Crane is in the Puzzle category
GCompris screenshots page states 'The panda is for puzzle activities, with all kinds of puzzle games to learn logic'

Unable to verify exact number of difficulty levels in Crane activity despite extensive web search of GCompris documentation, official website, GitHub repository, and developer blogs. The activity supports multiple progressive difficulty levels but exact count is not publicly documented.
Task configuration file specifies this path; confirmed in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh scripts which remove and check this file

pi_items.metadata_value: directional arrow buttons to control crane
pi_items.verified_value: directional arrow buttons (Up/Down/Left/Right) to move crane and items
pi_items.source: GCompris official screenshots and Stefan Toncu's Google Summer of Code blog post confirm 'four arrows that let you move the selected item'

pi_items.metadata_value: pick up colored items and place them to match the target pattern shown on the right
pi_items.verified_value: move items in blue frame to match their position in model frame
pi_items.source: GCompris official screenshots page: 'move the items in the blue frame to match their position in the model frame'

privileged_info_summary: GCompris is a real open-source educational software suite for children aged 2-10. The Crane is a verified activity within the Puzzle category created by Stefan Toncu. The activity involves manipulating items using directional arrow buttons to match target patterns shown in a reference frame. However, the Puzzle category icon is actually a panda holding jigsaw pieces, not a yellow puzzle piece as described in the task. The exact number of difficulty levels available in the Crane activity could not be verified from publicly available documentation.
